zh.lifeSummary: Jane De Bois於1145年出生在英國。她於大約1178年與Thomas de Bois在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1193年在英國肯特享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane De Bois于1145年出生在英国。她于大约1178年与Thomas de Bois在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1193年在英国肯特享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jane De Bois was born in 1145, in England. She married Thomas de Bois about 1178, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died about 1193, in Kent, England, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane De Bois est née en 1145, à Angleterre. Elle a épousé Thomas de Bois environ 1178, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1193, à Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jane De Bois wurde 1145 in England geboren. Sie hat um 1178 in England Thomas de Bois geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1193 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Kent, England.

it.lifeSummary: Jane De Bois è nata il nel 1145 nel England. Lei sposato Thomas de Bois circa 1178, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1193, nel Kent, England, all'età di 49 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane De Boisは1145年にイギリスで生まれた。約1178年にイギリスでThomas de Boisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1193年にイギリスケントで49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane De Bois(은)는 1145년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1178년 영국에서 Thomas de Bois(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1193년 영국 켄트에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane De Bois nasceu em 1145, em Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Thomas de Bois aproximadamente 1178, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1193, em Kent, Inglaterra, com 49 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane De Bois родилась в 1145 г. в местности Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas de Bois около 1178 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла около 1193 г. в местности кэнт, Англия в возрасте 49 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jane De Bois nació en 1145, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Thomas de Bois aproximadamente 1178, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente 1193, en Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 49 años.
